title: New Thassilon
summary: The recently returned Thassilonian civilization, formed by time-displaced peoples, is now split between the rule of Runelord Belimarius and the surprising reformer Runelord Sorshen.
content:
New Thassilon is the returned civilization of Ancient Thassilonian peoples — survivors and time-displaced subjects of the old empire who have re-established a presence in Varisia. It is currently split between the rule of two Runelords: Runelord Belimarius (Runelord of Envy) and Runelord Sorshen (Runelord of Lust), who has taken a surprisingly different path from her former colleagues.

The return of New Thassilon has sparked renewed interest in Thassilonian history and ruins throughout Varisia. It has also created a new wave of worship for two long-lost gods: Lissala (goddess of runes, once the patron deity of Thassilon) and the Peacock Spirit (a mysterious god of success and prosperity).

title: The Late Unpleasantness
summary: A series of dark events in 4702 AR — murders by the serial killer 'Chopper' and the fire that destroyed Sandpoint's original chapel — that the townspeople prefer not to speak of.
content:
Referred to locally as 'the Late Unpleasantness,' a series of catastrophic events struck Sandpoint in the winter of 4702 AR. First, a string of gruesome murders was carried out by a killer known only as 'Chopper' took the lives of dozens of townspeople before being stopped during his last terrible night.

Shortly afterward, a great fire destroyed the original Sandpoint chapel and the surrounding buildings, killing Father Ezakien Tobyn. Tobyn was succeeded by Abstalar Zantus, who took on the task of rebuilding the cathedral and the faith of the people of Sandpoint

title: Sandpoint's Founding Families
summary: Four powerful families from Magnimar — the Deverins, Scarnettis, Kaijitsus, and Valdemars — founded Sandpoint in 4666 AR, and their descendants continue to shape the town's politics today.
content:
In 4666 AR, four powerful families from Magnimar consolidated their resources and sailed north to found Sandpoint. Each family brought a different industry: the Kaijitsus were glassmakers and jewelers, the Valdemars shipbuilders and carpenters, the Scarnettis loggers and millers, and the Deverins farmers and brewers.

Their founding was marred almost immediately. They arrived to find the land already inhabited by a large Varisian tribe. After some failed negotiations, a scandal leaving dozens of Varisians and Scarnettis dead rocked the negotiations. The resulting crisis threatened the establishement of the town all together until a compromise was reached: the Varisians received a sixty-year share of Sandpoint profits (later extended to 4726 AR) and the promise of a Desnan temple in the cathedral.

Today, the Deverins and Scarnettis remain Sandpoint's most influential founding families. The Kaijitsus departed when Ameiko Kaijitsu ascended to become Empress of Minkai. The Valdemars have effectively withdrawn from town politics following financial setbacks and the death of their Sandpoint patriarch.

title: The Rise of the Runelords
summary: The emergence of Runelord Karzoug in Sandpoint.
content:
Runelord Karzoug emerged in Sandpoint in 4702 AR as the initial one of Thassilon's seven runelords to awaken from millennia of stasis, kept just beyond the fringes of reality until an unwitting stone giant wizard triggered the mechanism that facilitated the runelord's resurgence. A group of heroes rose against Karzoug and his formidable giant forces, ultimately defeating him in 4707 AR and thereby reviving the legacy of Thassilon. As the revelation spread across Avistan that Varisia had once been the domain of a potent and ancient wizard empire that had faded into history, adventurers, scholars, explorers, and researchers arrived from distant lands to explore and delve into the enigma of Thassilon.

Leaders among Varisia's city-states became increasingly concerned. Seven runelords had vanished during Earthfall, and if the upheaval caused by a single awakening was so catastrophic, the potential ramifications of all the runelords rising from their slumber were ominous. Responding to their concerns, a fresh group of heroes embarked on a quest to locate and reconstruct an ancient artifact, the seven-pointed Sihedron, piecing it together from its fractured remnants. Originally crafted by Thassilon's founder, the formidable wizard Xin, the Sihedron housed the secrets of Thassilonian magic and stood as a crucial defense against the awakening of the other runelords, should such a scenario arise.

title: The Return of the Runelords
summary: The remaining Runelords begin to awaken from their slumbers.
content: In 4718 AR, after awakening from her slumber, Runelord Alaznist employed the Scepter of Ages to travel back in time and replace the history of Golarion with one where she rules over a New Thassilon stretching from the Lands of the Linnorm Kings to Ustalav and Nidal and beyond. Her efforts were foiled by a group of adventurers working under her rival Sorshen. By the time Alaznist was defeated, most runelords were also dead, except Sorshen and Belimarius, who claimed the land that would become New Thassilon.
